project_fields.project_type: Open-source developer tool and local interactive demo
project_fields.tech_stack: Python 3.11+, SQLite, standard-library HTTP server, vanilla JavaScript, OpenAI Responses API
project_fields.use_cases: Long-running agents, developer workflows, auditable context selection, scoped memory, reproducible AI evaluation
project_fields.capability: Application-layer context governance: lifecycle and scope gates, dependency-complete selection, visible conflicts, strict estimated-token budgets, deterministic reason traces, and reproducible offline evaluation.
project_fields.openai_models_and_apis: OpenAI Responses API with GPT-5.6 Sol for the optional live path. The deterministic compiler, local demo, and benchmark run without a model or network connection.
project_fields.other_models_or_apis: No other model API is required or used by the reviewed release.
project_fields.building_process: Codex helped inspect the repository, implement and red-team the deterministic compiler, expand tests, generate release artifacts, and verify public claims. The final package preserves every failure and separates offline contract evidence, one API-path smoke test, and one 24-sample model-behavior observation.
project_fields.setup_steps: Install Python 3.11+. Download the release wheel, then run: python -m pip install acheon_context-0.1.0-py3-none-any.whl; acheon demo; acheon serve --port 8000. Open http://127.0.0.1:8000. The default demo is local and needs no API key.
project_fields.title: Acheon: Auditable Context for Long-Running AI Workflows
project_fields.tagline: A deterministic control layer that turns noisy histories into bounded, inspectable context while preserving current constraints, dependencies, and unresolved conflicts.
project_fields.description:
Acheon is an open-source application-layer context governance compiler for long-running AI workflows. It stores typed, versioned records; excludes revoked, expired, superseded, and out-of-scope material; preserves required dependencies and visible conflicts; and emits a bounded canonical context packet with a reason code for every selection decision.

The offline core includes a local UI, equal-budget controls, ablations, full failures, a 240-case synthetic selection benchmark, and a separate 24-case model-behavior eval. An optional Responses API adapter sends the compiled packet to GPT-5.6 Sol with stored history treated as untrusted data. Acheon does not modify model weights, expand a provider context window, or claim permanent model memory. Current evidence establishes engineering contracts and high-impact potential, not an Acheon-attributable answer-quality improvement.
